title: brachyglottis kirkii
intro: Brachyglottis kirkii, also known as the Kirk's coprosma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including soothing sore throats, relieving digestive issues, and reducing fever. The Maori have traditionally used it in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, teas, and poultices, to harness its medicinal properties.
